title: The page does not calculate
steps.0.text: Open `script.js` and print any number to the console.
steps.1.text: Make it the total: 1247 coffees at 3 lv each. The sum is written in the code — a ready answer does not count.
checkLabels.t7: The sum is in the code
why.empty: script.js is empty. That is where you write in this lesson.
hint2.empty: Open the script.js tab and write one line.
why.untouched: script.js is still empty. That is where you write in this lesson.
hint2.untouched: Open the script.js tab. The other files are not touched.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error before it got to the end.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel on the right. The red line says what it did not like, and where.
hint3.crashed: The most common one on day one is a missing bracket or quote. A line starting with console.log(42 and no closing bracket stops everything after it.
why.nothing-written: script.js is empty. There is nothing to run.
why.frozen: Your code never stops. It loops forever and was cut off.
hint2.frozen: One line is enough in this lesson. If you wrote a loop, remove it — you do not need it yet.
why.no-output: The code ran, but nothing came out. The console is empty.
hint2.no-output: `console.log` is what shows things. Without it the sum is worked out and thrown away.
hint3.no-output: Write only 12 + 30 and the computer works out 42 and forgets it at once. Nobody asked it to show it.
why.wrong-number: A number came out, but not that one. The total is different.
hint2.wrong-number: The multiplication sign in JavaScript is not x. The letter x is a name, not an action.
hint4.wrong-number: Do the same with the two numbers from the page — how many coffees, and at what price.
why.typed-answer: The number is right, but it is written by hand. The computer worked out nothing — it only repeated what you already knew.
hint2.typed-answer: The point of the lesson is for the sum to be in the code. Leave the two numbers and the sign between them, not the result.
blocks.1.text: Look at the page on the right. Coffees sold: 1247. Price: 3 lv. Total: 1247 x 3. That is not a sum. Those are characters. The page shows exactly what you wrote into it, letter by letter.
blocks.2.text: HTML repeats. It does not calculate. There is no tag for multiply, no tag for remember, no tag for if. CSS will not help either — it decides how the text looks, not what it says.
blocks.4.text: JavaScript performs actions. It does not describe the page — it does things with it. It lives in a separate file, `script.js`, and runs from top to bottom, line by line, the moment the page opens. Here is the first line you will write:
blocks.5.marks.0.label: SHOWS
blocks.5.marks.1.label: WHAT
blocks.5.legend.0: The semicolon at the end says the line is over. Leave it out and JavaScript usually works it out — but not always.
blocks.6.items.0: `console` is the console — the place where your code can tell you something.
blocks.6.items.1: `log` is the action: show. The dot between them means "on the console, do log".
blocks.6.items.2: Inside the brackets goes whatever you want to see. Here it is the sum `12 + 30`.
blocks.7.text: Notice what comes out. Not `12 + 30`, but 42. The sum happens BEFORE the showing — only the result reaches the console.
blocks.9.text: Not on the page. The number goes to the console — the panel on the right.
blocks.11.text: The preview and the console are two different places. The preview is what a visitor sees. The console is what you see while you write.
blocks.12.text: That is why the console is the first thing in this course. From here on, every error goes through it.
blocks.13.text: What can go in the brackets
blocks.14.text: Not only sums. A number, a piece of text, anything:
blocks.16.text: Text needs quotes. Without them JavaScript takes it for the name of something and looks for where it was declared — and there is nothing. A number needs no quotes.
blocks.18.text: The computer does not show what you did not ask for. This line is perfectly valid and does nothing visible:
blocks.20.text: The sum is worked out. The result is thrown away at once, because nobody asked for it. The console stays empty.
blocks.22.text: The code runs from top to bottom. Three lines give three lines:
blocks.24.quote: The computer does not guess. It does not calculate in its head and does nothing you did not tell it explicitly. It sounds like a flaw. It is the only reason code is predictable.
blocks.26.items.0: HTML shows characters. JavaScript performs actions.
blocks.26.items.1: `console.log` shows something in the console, not on the page.
blocks.26.items.2: Inside the brackets goes the number, text or sum you want to see.
blocks.26.items.3: Without `console.log` the sum is worked out and forgotten.
